desc:
This area is strewn with broken rocks of various sizes that have fallen from
above. Some of the rubble here isn't much larger than coarse sand, while other
chunks are as big as a boulder. It might be possible to dig through some of the
stones laying about, but it doesn't seem like there is much to find other than
more rocks.
name: Rubble-filled Breach

desc:
A long corridor coming from the east ends abruptly here. Warm, wet steam drifts
up from an opening below, carrying with it a strong sulfurous odour. Cobwebs
swirl around, mixing with the wet air and creating sticky clumps that fall and
cover the floor.
name: Dead End

desc:
Solid stone has been split somehow, creating a deep cleft. The air here is thick
with the smell of sulphur and rot. The walls are rough, black rock, and the
floor is jagged and treacherous. There seems to be a passage to the east. From
the west, there is the sound of trickling water and the feel of hot, humid air.
name: Dark Fissure

desc:
A fissure in the rock that runs east and west here. Moist, hot vapour can be
felt coming from below, making the air feel thick and heavy. A rancid-smelling
runnel of water is running down the wall from some unseen source above,
disappearing near the edge of a large boulder below.
name: Dark Fissure

desc:
A deep fissure in the solid stone seems to run east-west. The air is thick with
the smell of sulphur and rot. The floor is jagged and treacherous, and the walls
are rough, black rock. From the east, there is the sound of trickling water, and
hot, humid air can be felt coming from that direction.
name: Dark Fissure

desc:
A fissure ends abruptly here and meets a steep, rough tunnel coming from above.
The way west is blocked by rocks that seem to have fallen from the tunnel above.
Foul, moist air can be felt coming from the east, indicating possible passage
there. Loose rocks and gravel make walking treacherous.
name: Bottom of a Steep Tunnel

desc:
A cramped tunnel slopes steeply upward. The rough, natural walls show no
evidence of workmanship or tools. The air is heavy, and wet, condensing into a
sticky slime on the walls. There is a draught of warm, wet air moving up through
the tunnel from below.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
A rough tunnel extends nearly vertically below, but seems to level off to the
south. The wet walls are slick, making it difficult to find hand- or foot-
holds. There is steady movement of air through this tunnel from below, but it is
wet and sticky, with a thick, acrid smell.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
This passageway seems to be some kind of entrance. There is evidence of heavy,
though not recent, travel through here. The air is less foul here; likely due to
the rockslide that seems to have blocked most of the passage to the east. This
passageway continues to the south.
name: Rough Passageway

desc:
This cramped cave seems to have been used as a den by some creature long ago.
The stone floor here has been worn smooth, but otherwise the cave is
featureless. There is a large pile of bones, petrified dung, and other refuse in
the corner, covered in what must be years dust and dirt. It is clear that
whatever lived here, it was a long time ago, but there is no evidence of why
they left nor where they have gone.
name: Vacant Den

desc:
This is a shallow pit, eroded from the stone by a constant trickle of rank water
from above. Thick sludge, created by decades of sediment, covers the floor and
seems to absorb the rill coming from above. Steam with a heavy, acrid, sulphur
smell bubbles up through the muck every now and again from some unseen fissure
in the rock below. The only way out is up.
name: Small Hollow in the Stone

desc:
It is difficult to sense size in the oppressive darkness, but this area somehow
seems a bit more open than other parts of this cave complex. The floor is
exceedingly uneven here, sloping downward to the north, sharply upwards to the
west, and slightly upwards towards the east. There is a strong smell of rot,
both vegetation and animal, coming from the west, mixing with a musty smell from
the east, and an acrid, sulphur smell from the south.
name: Rough Intersection

desc:
Stale, heavy air saturates this dead-end tunnel. In the darkness, it is
difficult to discern what lies beyond in either direction, though there is a
sense of expansiveness to the west. The air coming from that direction is
slightly acrid and has a faint odour of decay, a sharp contrast to the musty
smells here.
name: Musty Tunnel

desc:
A narrow passageway runs east-west here. The walls are wet and covered in some
sort of sticky slime. The rough floor slopes upwards to the west, and is slick
with the same slime coating the walls. Warm, wet air can be felt from the east,
and the putrid smell hangs heavily in the air.
name: Passageway

desc:
The rough passageway runs steeply up and down here, but there is a sense of an
opening to the east. The smell from below is foetid, though the wet, rank air
here is little better. The floor is jagged and uneven, with large chunks of
solid stone staggered at irregular intervals. The stones form a sort of rugged
staircase, making the passage up and down this tunnel possible, though still
hazardous.
name: Rough Steps

desc:
The twisting and turning tunnel curves to the west and to the south. The floor
of the tunnel is level as it continues westward, but climbs drastically upward
to the south, with natural stone steps carved into it. The floor is dusty, and
there are piles of rocks lining the bottoms of the walls on either side of the
tunnel.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Twisting and turning, the tunnel leads to the west and south. The floor to the
west slopes down drastically, while the floor levels off to the south. The walls
are smooth, glossy, and brightly reflect any light that touches them. Rocks and
pebbles line the floor, creating hazardous passage in either direction.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel twists sharply and suddenly to the north and the east. Passage is
difficult in either direction, as large rocks line the floor of the tunnel. The
walls are smooth and cold to the touch. Water drips from the smooth ceiling
above, and the air is damp and humid.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel continues to twist and turn to the west and to the south. Stone
stairs lead upward to the south, where the cavern begins to open up. Loose
gravel and rocks line the floor and form large piles along the walls and passage
in either direction is quite difficult. In ironic contrast, the walls and
ceiling are quite smooth.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends to the west and south. There is a steep stone stair to the west
that leads down into the dark tunnel below. All about the tunnel here are grey
and black rocks and boulders of various sizes. There is a large pile of rubble
stacked against the wall to the south, but opening a passage there should be no
problem.
name: Rock-filled Passage

desc:
The floor of this shallow pit is covered with thick, sticky muck that gives off
a foetid smell, rank with death and rot. It is dark and wet here, and
unnaturally quiet, as if the very rock is absorbing any sound. The only way out
of this pit is to climb through the hole above.
name: Filthy Pit

desc:
Huge rectangular rocks, tilted at various angles, are arranged like a large
staircase that has partially collapsed on one side, seemingly from huge force
from above. A deep jagged crack runs down the centre, and the stones to the east
of that are cracked and crumbling. The west side still looks passable.
name: Broken Stairs

desc:
Huge chunks of solid stone staggered at irregular intervals form a sort of
rugged staircase running up and down. Passage is possible, but challenging. The
air is wet and heavy, with a mixture of foul smells. It is hard to tell what is
worse, the smells from below or the smells from above. Every now and then,
muffled sounds can be heard in the distance, echoing through the caves and
tunnels.
name: Rough Steps

desc:
A passageway comes to an abrupt end here, though sounds can be heard coming from
the west. A foetid smell fills the air, a mixture of rotting flesh and
composting plants. The moisture in the air condenses on the walls, which are
covered with moss and mould.
name: Dead End

desc:
This space is cramped and small, and exceedingly dark. There is a sense of an
immense opening to the north. The walls and floor are covered with a thick,
sticky slime, and a foul stench permeates the air. Shuffling and scraping sounds
can be heard echoing all around.
name: Cramped Entrance

desc:
It is oppressively dark, but there is a sense of an expansive chasm extending to
the northwest. The smell of mould and rot fills the dank air, and the floor is
covered with a thick sludge. From the sounds coming from somewhere, it is clear
that this grotto is inhabited, but the darkness and the echoes make it
impossible to distinguish where, or what these inhabitants might be.
name: Huge Grotto

desc:
This huge grotto is rank with foul odours, and the air is wet and heavy. The
thick sludge covering the ground is treacherous to walk on. Sounds echo from all
around, and movement can be sensed somewhere nearby. Mould and slime cover the
walls.
name: Huge Grotto

desc:
Wet, slick slime covers the floor, which slopes sharply upward to the south. A
narrow shaft opens below, and odd sounds can be heard from that direction every
now and again. The cool wet air is rank with the smell of rotting plants and
mould.
name: Top of a Steep Shaft

desc:
This cave is shaped like a giant dome. There are a few cracks in the roof, which
allow just a bit of the outside world into this dark and desolate place. Brown,
slimy water drips through the cracks, making the floor slick and wet. Strange
sounds and smells come from the north.
name: Dome-shaped Cave

desc:
The floor opens up into a large, vast cavern. A huge, massive rock rests
directly in the centre of the floor, providing a great vantage point. The walls
climb up toward the ceiling some thirty feet overhead, where stalactites hang
down into the darkness. Looking closely, a small opening can be spotted in the
centre of the ceiling, impossible to reach without being able to fly up to it.
name: Wide Open Cavern

desc:
With piles of dried leaves and grasses formed into neat beds, it is apparent
that this lair is inhabited by the hive, perhaps an outcast band. The beds are
in close proximity to one another, leaving not much room to navigate between
them or throughout the rest of this tight, cramped space.
name: Cramped Lair

desc:
The cavern here is vast and huge, where the walls are steep and vertical. The
floor is mostly level and clear of debris. Stalagmites rise from the floor and
form giant grey columns. A foul smell fills the cavern from wall to wall, and
swarms of flies buzz all about.
name: Wide Cavern Opening

desc:
The floor of the tunnel drops off suddenly to the east, where it leads down into
a dark corridor. In the other direction, the tunnel opens into a wide, vast
cavern. Stalagmites form large grey columns here, and have a thick, black liquid
running down them.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Blankets of some grey cave fungi cover the walls and ceiling of the cave.
Salamanders and geckos scurry over them, chasing after insects that have been
attracted to the musty smell. A black liquid also runs down the walls and coats
the fungi in places. The blanket of fungi comes to an end on the walls toward
the south, where they become grey again and open up into a vast cavern.
name: Musty Lair

desc:
The floor makes a sudden, steep ascent to the west while it continues on before
becoming level to the east. The walls are formed from a rough, jagged, grey
basalt stone, and seem to have some foul, black liquid running down them. Flies
swarm all about, and seem to be particularly attracted to the liquid.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
The centre of the floor is worn smooth and dark, indicating that the corridor is
well-travelled. Along the sides, rubble has been pushed and now lie in small
piles. The air is warm and humid, making breathing difficult. A foul odour wafts
in from the west, and flies buzz about and cover the grey basalt walls.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
The ground climbs gradually from east to west, and the floor becomes smoother as
it ascends. Piles of debris and rubble lie pushed against the walls. The air
filling the corridor is warm and humid, and flies buzz all about. The walls are
formed from jagged, grey basalt stone.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
The tunnel splits off into three directions here. The tunnel to the west climbs
gradually upward into the pitch black darkness. The tunnel to the south leads
downward into the darkness there, with a musty odour emanating from that
direction. To the east is a straight, level tunnel.
name: Tunnel Junction

desc:
The floor of the tunnel rises upward to the north into the vast darkness there.
There is a small opening in the eastern rock wall that opens into a larger side
cave. To the south, the tunnel continues onward into the darkness, with a foul
odour drifting in from there.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The walls are narrow, and formed of a jagged, grey basalt stone. The air here is
dry and stale, with dust particles floating all throughout. The floor is dry and
dusty, with dust stirring up underfoot with each footstep. A single, large
stalagmite rises up from the centre of the floor and connects to the ceiling,
seemingly forming a column to support it.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The narrow tunnel continues along east and west. Small piles of rubble have
collected along the bottoms of the walls. The floor is dry and dusty, showing no
signs of wear or heavy travel. The air here is light and dry with dust particles
floating all about.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The walls of the tunnel are made of gritty, grey basalt rock. The straight,
narrow tunnel runs east and west. There is a large pile of rubble to the east,
where the tunnel abruptly stops in that direction. The smooth tunnel floor runs
off into the darkness to the west.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnels open from all directions here into a wide, high-ceiling cavern. The
floor of the cavern is covered in rocks and gravel, and dips down in multiple
spots. There is a small opening carved into the western wall, which leads to a
tight, narrow cave. The air is warm and carries with it a foul stench.
name: Wide Cavern

desc:
All around the cave is grey basalt rock. The walls, floor, and ceiling are all
covered with the same grainy texture. Large stalactites hang from the ceiling,
dripping a disgusting black liquid, which forms a large puddle in the corner of
the cave below it. There is a small opening in the cave wall to the west.
name: Side Cave

desc:
Whether natural or man-made, this side cave is carved into the wall of the wide
cavern to the east. The ceiling is low and stalactites hang in the darkness.
There are piles of rocks and large boulders lining the walls of the cave. The
air is warm, dry and dusty, and a foul odour fills the space from wall to wall.
name: Side Cave

desc:
The tunnel opens up into a large, wide space as it curves north and west here.
The walls are jagged with small rocks protruding all about. The floor is dusty,
and it slopes down gradually to the west, where the tunnel continues into the
darkness. A foul smell lingers here.
name: Long, Wide Tunnel

desc:
The tunnels intersect into three different directions here. Two tunnels branch
off to the east, one leading upward into the darkness and the other leading
down. The dusty floor to the west continues its gradual descent as the tunnel
seems to never end in that direction.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
All about, loose pebbles and gravel lie strewn about with no particular
consistency. Rocks of all shapes and sizes protrude from the grey basalt walls,
which go up into the darkness and form a curved ceiling. The air is heavy and
carries a foul odour with it.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel makes a sudden, sharp turn to the east and south. The floor of the
tunnel becomes steep as it leads downward to the south, and comes to a set of
grey basalt stairs, which seems to have been naturally carved into the floor.
The jagged rocks protruding from the walls lends a sturdy and reliable means to
hold onto in order to maintain balance. A heavy, putrid odour fills the tunnel.
name: Long, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The walls are close together, with jagged rocks of various sizes protruding out,
slightly impeding travel in either direction. The floor is well-worn and smooth,
and the air becomes warmer and more humid to the east. The tunnel continues in
that direction.
name: Narrow, Twisting Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is narrow, and the ceiling is no more than six feet overhead. The air
is heavy and humid, and condensation has collected on the walls. The ceiling
ascends toward the west, but the tunnel remains just as narrow. It comes to an
end to the east, as it opens into a vast cavern.
name: Cramped Tunnel

desc:
The cavern is vast and spacious. In the centre of the floor is a small, clear
pond, the water supplied by dripping from the large stalactites hanging from the
ceiling above. The ceiling is high, and there is a rock ledge jutting out from
the walls above, with a steep ramp providing access there. Two very small and
cramped tunnels lead out of the cavern from either direction.
name: Vast Basalt Cavern

desc:
Connecting two vast caverns, the crudely carved tunnel is small and cramped,
with a low, jagged ceiling. The smooth floor descends down to the east, where it
comes to an abrupt ending, and opens up into a huge cavern below. Sliding down
the wall to the cavern below would not be a hassle, but the tunnel is too high
to climb back up to.
name: Tunnel Leading Down

desc:
The smooth grey walls and rounded ceiling of this tunnel appear as if they were
carved intentionally. The floor is smooth, the air is warm and humid, and a
putrid smell lingers here. The foot of some natural stone stairs is here, and
climbing them would seem to be an easy task with the help of some jagged rocks
in the wall of the stairway that could provide reliable handholds.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
The tunnel here is more like a dwarven hall, with the smooth walls and rounded
ceiling seeming to be more intentional than natural. Faint screeches and
shuffling can be heard here, leading to the suspicion that there is some form of
life nearby. The smell here is overwhelmingly disgusting, the mixture of body
odour and sewage.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
The warm, humid air and the foul stench here indicates that something is near,
whether living or dead. The ceiling of this cavern disappears out of sight, into
the darkness above. There is a large basalt corridor to the north, while a
smaller tunnel branches off to the east.
name: Vast Basalt Cavern

desc:
Tunnels merge here from multiple directions. Large, grey stalactites hang from
above, dripping with a thick, black liquid. There are blood stains on the walls
and floor, and a trail of dried blood leading into an opening to the south
indicates that something sinister may lurk there.
name: Twisting Tunnel

desc:
A cave-in has completely blocked off this tunnel from continuing any more to the
east. Large rocks and boulders are piled densely, while the ceiling above is
cracked and seems very unstable. Bats seem to have claimed this area as their
own nesting grounds, as they hang in the dark, screeching constantly.
name: Partially Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
Branching off of the main corridor, the tunnel continues to the west before
twisting again to the south. The air here is oddly more humid than the other
nearby tunnels, so much to the point where moisture has collected on the walls,
occasionally running down. The air, though heavy, smells just as foul.
name: Humid Corridor

desc:
The walls are smooth and shiny, and the ceiling is rounded. This short corridor
leads north and south. The floor is dry and dusty, and there is a dark, smooth,
well-worn trail in the centre that runs the entire length of the corridor, an
indication of heavy foot traffic.
name: Basalt Corridor

desc:
A wide, grey basalt stairway leads up into the darkness to the north. The centre
of each stair is dark, smooth, and well-worn. The stairs are crude, and seem to
be naturally formed. Loud, obnoxious screeches can occasionally be heard coming
from above, and a horrible odour lingers.
name: Foot of Some Basalt Stairs

desc:
Dried blood stains the walls and floor all over the cavern here. Piles of bones
and skulls of various sizes lie strewn about. The putrid odour is overwhelming
and foul. Large, sinister-looking stalagmites shoot up from the ground like
columns, stained a rusted colour to match the walls and floors.
name: Blood-stained Cavern

desc:
The tunnel comes to a sudden end, with a large opening in the southern wall,
framed by large rocks. The floor is smooth, well-worn, and stained dark with
dried blood. There is a small rock ledge that has formed at the bottom of the
opening. Outside, a sheer rock cliff drops down toward the surrounding terrain
below. Entering the tunnel from that direction appears impossible without the
ability to fly.
name: Tunnel Opening

desc:
Along the stairs, a large opening is carved into the western wall. The floor is
smooth and well-worn, and blood stains the ground. The foul odour of death and
decay emanates from the west, while the natural basalt stairs continue upward
and down.
name: Basalt Stairs

desc:
Dried spatters of blood coat the lower portions of the walls and floor. A foul,
heavy odour indicates that death and decay are nearby. The rib bones of some
small animals, along with skulls of various sizes, lie haphazardly scattered
along the bases of the walls. The cave continues to the south, deeper into the
lair, while turning back to the east would provide a way out.
name: Blood-stained Lair

desc:
Deep within the cavernous lair, the air is heavy and malodorous. Dark, dried
blood stains the floor and walls, and bones lie stacked in small, scattered
piles. The bones are mostly small, and there are some full ribcages with dried,
decaying skin still stuck to them. Skulls of various shapes and sizes lie piled
among the blood and gore as well. The blood-stained walls are jagged and uneven,
and the walls of the lair narrow as they venture further into the darkness.
name: Bloody Lair

desc:
The very back of the lair is surprisingly clear of debris and gore. Large, lofty
beds are formed out of piles of dead and dried vegetation, where the members of
the hive sleep. The dirt that covers the ground is dry and blood-red. Some
well-worn paths are evident in that the dirt is pushed aside in some areas,
exposing the bare stone floor beneath it.
name: Back of a Lair

desc:
The stairs form a great means for which to travel upward or down. They are
crude, naturally carved, and steep. The centre of the steps are dark, smooth,
and well-travelled. There is no real consistency to the formation of the stairs.
Some of them are wide and smooth while others are narrow and rough.
name: Basalt Stairs

desc:
Wide, flat steps make passage on this part of the stairs very easy. The top of
the stairs is near overhead, but caverns split off into the walls on the sides.
The air here is evil and foul, and a terrible, overpowering stench is prevalent.
Piles of debris and waste pushed up against the walls indicate that something
large inhabits this place.
name: Wide Basalt Stairs

desc:
The floor is flat and level, but to the south, a steep set of natural stairs
leads down into the darkness below. A vile, foul smell emanates from that
direction, indicating that something terrible could lurk below. The corridor
opens up to the north, where the floor gradually climbs upward.
name: Top of Some Basalt Stairs

desc:
This large cave, naturally cut into the side wall of the stairs to the east, is
being used as a waste repository for the hive. Piles of debris lie here and
there, in the centre of the cave and against the walls. There is a wide enough
path cut through the piles of debris, making passage possible. Guano, both old
and fresh, has been left here in heaping mounds as well.
name: Piles of Guano

desc:
Small and cramped, this cave forms a sleeping lair for the hive. The walls climb
high toward the ceiling above, and all along them, shelves jut out where beds of
dried vegetation have been made for the creatures to sleep in. The dirt that
covers the stone floor is blood-red, but dry and dusty. There are well-worn
paths that have formed in the dirt.
name: Small, Cramped Lair

desc:
A wide, high ceiling and clean, grey basalt walls form the corridor that leads
to the cavernous lair nearby. The floor is well-worn and mostly clean, save the
occasional clump of dirt that has been tracked here. A disgusting odour fills
the corridor from wall to wall.
name: Threshhold of a Vast Lair

desc:
A small set of natural stairs formed from wide, flat rocks leads up to a vast
cavern where the hive dwells in one direction, and provide for a means of egress
in the other. The air here is heavy and warm and flies buzz about. Stalagmites
rise up from the cavern floor and disappear into the vast darkness toward the
ceiling above.
name: Basalt Lair

desc:
Large stalagmites climb toward the ceiling from the floor, and dry, dead
vegetation covers areas of the floor, packed tightly to form large beds. The
ambient temperature here is noticeably high, and the vast quantity of beds
indicate that a large population of some primitive species populates this lair.
name: Basalt Lair

desc:
A collection of stalagmites, formed tightly together, sits neatly near the
corner of the cavern. All about, piles of dried and dead vegetation lie
meticulously formed into large, comfortable beds. Paths have been carefully
formed between all of the bedding, with the red dirt pushed away to expose the
bare stone floor beneath it.
name: Corner of a Lair

desc:
Deep within the vast cavern, more piles of bedding lie all about. Wide, flat
rocks jut out all along the walls, forming shelves upon which more beds lie. On
the floor below, well-worn pathways weave throughout the lair between the beds,
which seems to suggest that they are very well travelled.
name: Depths of a Basalt Lair

desc:
Well-worn pathways snake throughout the cavern, between large and small piles of
bedding where the creatures sleep. Rock shelves protrude from the walls, upon
which more beds are formed. The air is hot and humid, and the ground is covered
with a thick layer of dry, blood-red dirt in which the pathways are formed.
name: Basalt Lair

desc:
The floor is flat and slopes down toward the south, while it twists upward to
the east. The air is warm, humid, and smells foul. The floor is well-worn in the
centre, and the walls are crude and jagged. Scratch marks can be discerned along
the walls upon close inspection.
name: Wide Basalt Corridor

desc:
A steep ramp leads up this large, flat ledge, high above the cavern below. The
large stalactites are close, and it is evident that the clear water dripping
from them is feeding the pond below. Geckos crawl on the walls here, but scurry
off at the sound of footsteps.
name: Rocky Ledge

desc:
Boulders and large rocks partially fill the tunnel, while smaller piles of
rubble and pebbles line the floor along the walls. The passage to the north is
slightly obstructed by two large boulders, but manoeuvring around them is quite
possible. The floor of the tunnel to the south descends suddenly and is very
steep.
name: Steep Tunnel

desc:
As it descends downward into the vast darkness, the tunnel becomes more narrow.
Ascending upward, the tunnel opens up and becomes filled with more loose rocks
and pebbles. The steep walls and floor are made of a gritty, grey basalt rock.
On the eastern wall, a large crack has formed and contains a thick, black
liquid.
name: Steep, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is narrow and cramped, and slopes steeply downward into complete
darkness. The ground is covered with a layer of fine dust and the air here is
heavy to breathe. There is a large, wide crack in the grey stone of the eastern
wall that seems to run off into the darkness.
name: Tunnel Descending into Darkness

desc:
The tunnel is steep and descends into the darkness below. The grey walls and
ceiling narrow downward as they go. A large crack on the eastern wall is covered
in a foul, black liquid that glistens when light touches it. The air seems to
become more difficult to breathe further down the tunnel, while going up would
have the opposite effect.
name: Tunnel Descending into Darkness

desc:
The floor of the tunnel here is level. There is a tight, narrow opening that
leads up into a dark, steep tunnel. The tunnel bends sharply to the south, and
the faint sound of splashing water can be heard. The air is damp, heavy, and
humid. The walls are covered with drops of water that occasionally run down in
streams.
name: Dark Tunnel Bend

desc:
Tunnels open into this huge cavern from the north and the west. The walls are
almost entirely vertical and naturally carved out of grey, columnar stone. They
disappear into the impenetrable darkness above. The sound of splashing water
comes from the east, where the cavern continues into the darkness. To the south,
a natural ramp made of grey gravel slopes upward.
name: Vast Dark Cavern

desc:
The tunnel slopes down toward the west. The ground and walls here are damp and
the ground is covered in grey gravel. To the west, the faint sound of splashing
can occasionally be heard. There is a foul odour carried by air flowing from
that direction. On the ceiling, small dry stalactites protrude downward.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The path leads in from the north and continues onto a small ledge, just wide
enough for two people to pass side by side. Rocks jut out of the cave wall from
the west and occasionally impede passage, making it difficult to continue along
the path as it leads southward. To the east, a large black lake sits about
fifteen feet below the ledge.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
This is a large cavern with grey, columnar stone walls that ascend up into the
endless darkness above. A small opening is visible in the eastern wall, with a
ramp made of gravel leading up to it. Occasional drips can be heard coming from
the west, and the air here is heavy and humid. Two large boulders stand side by
side to the north, with just enough room to seemingly squeeze between.
name: Cavern Opening

desc:
The walls of the cavern are grey and made of columnar stone, with a foul black
liquid dripping down the northern and western walls. There are some small, short
stalactites hanging from the ceiling above. In the corner of the cavern is a
collection of dust, gravel, and some tiny animal bones.
name: Dark, Damp Cavern

desc:
The cavern wall makes a sudden turn to the west, where it disappears into the
damp darkness. Some inky black liquid runs onto the floor of the cavern from the
west. The air is damp, heavy, and musty. Black and green mosses line the grey
stone walls. Every now and then, a critter scurries across the ground and
plunges into the black pool of water.
name: Dark, Damp Cavern

desc:
The ceiling of the cave is low, with stalactites protruding down and the surface
of a black, foul pool of water is only three feet below. This is the centre of
the pool that is flooding the cavern, and it is deep enough that the blackness
of the water within conceals the bottom.
name: Flooded Tunnel

desc:
The ledge makes a turn toward the east, where it continues into the darkness.
The ceiling here slopes low toward the wall to the west and south. The ledge
rises up further from the surface of the lake below, and the path is dry and
dusty. Grey stones jut out of the walls, making it difficult to walk upright in
some spots.
name: Narrow Ledge above a Dark Lake

desc:
The cave here is flooded with a deep pool of black water. Stalactites hang from
the ceiling and drip with a steady flow of black foul water that continually
fills the pool below. The water is stagnant and foul-smelling, causing the air
here to be heavy and musty. Small fish occasionally break the surface of the
water with a splash.
name: Flooded Tunnel

desc:
A thick, brown mud mixes with the black water that floods the tunnels to create
a disgusting substance in this small corner cave. Knee-deep and with the stench
or rot and decay, the mud makes manoeuvring very difficult. The walls of the
cave are brown with caked-on mud, and a black water ring seems to permanently
stain them.
name: Muddy Pool

desc:
The pool of water to the north and east suddenly comes to a stop here, where the
ground is still damp but not flooded. All about, salamanders scurry and chase
after small insects. Tiny carcasses of small fish lie scattered about, with
flies buzzing about and maggots crawling around. There are black footprints that
lead from the direction of the water and into the depths of the caves beyond.
name: Wet Cave

desc:
The walls to the south and east have large, gaping cracks in which cool, fresh
air can be felt flowing through. The air is dry and makes breathing much easier.
Salamanders crawl all about the floor and walls, chasing after small, unseen
insects. The ground here is dry and covered with a fine dust.
name: Back of a Cave

desc:
Salamanders cover the walls of this nook in the cavern. They scatter at the
slightest disturbance. Rotten vegetation covers the top halves of the walls and
roots cover the ceiling, dripping with a black liquid. The putrid air here is
heavy and difficult to breathe, and flies buzz all about. There are large cracks
in the stone floor underfoot.
name: Corner of the Cave

desc:
The dark liquid is captured in the corner of the cavern here, forming a deep,
black pool. The liquid is solid black and shows no signs of transparency
whatsoever. The ceiling slopes down to the east and south, and the water almost
touches it. There are occasional splashes in the water, indicating lots of
activity from the fish below the surface.
name: Flooded Tunnel

desc:
This cave has a very high ceiling, with large stalagmites protruding from the
ground. The walls in every direction are lined with large piles of grey, damp
gravel. Green and black moss grows prevalently here, thoroughly covering the
stones and beginning their ascent up the walls. There is an opening in the cave
wall high above, which seems too steep to climb to.
name: Dead End Cave

desc:
Cold, wet, and muddy, this dark hole has been created under the cavern floor
above. Whether it was created intentionally or naturally is unknown. Various
insects inhabit this place, a great source of food for the plethora of
salamanders that are found down in this area of the caves.
name: Dark, Muddy Hole

desc:
The rugged, natural stairway that runs up and down here is traversed by a huge
crack. The crack is large enough to pass through to the west, but blocked by
debris to the east. A foul stench and the occasional indistinct, but unsettling
sound comes from the west.
name: Cleft in the Stairs

desc:
There is evidence that, at some point, something used this small cave as a home.
Bones, white with age, and other rubbish are piled all around. The floor has
been worn smooth, and there is a faint smell of ash. It seems as though whatever
lived here left, or died a very long time ago.
name: Vacant Den
